title: Help Purchasing Neon Genesis Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Help Purchasing Neon Genesis Evangelion             Quote Post by Bonifatus » Wed Feb 18, 2015 1:31 pm I'm a fairly new fan to the series and was looking to purchase Neon Genesis on DVD. Due to price constraints I'm leaning towards the Platinum Holiday Edition. I was wondering what I should look for to ensure I'm not buying a Bootleg.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Wed Feb 18, 2015 3:34 pm If I were you, I'd just save my money for the Blu Ray release, which is right around the corner in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Feb 18, 2015 3:58 pm ^ This is definitely an option, but only if he understands Japanese since there most likely won't be English subtitles on that release, let alone the dub. But I guess, Funimation will release the blu-ray version eventually, so if you can wait, Bonifatus, you will be better served with that release, imho.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Feb 18, 2015 4:16 pm Waiting seems to be the modern Eva way, in my opinion.

[]: Re: Help Purchasing Neon Genesis             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 18, 2015 4:27 pm Bonifatus wrote: I'm leaning towards the Platinum Holiday Edition. I was wondering what I should look for to ensure I'm not buying a Bootleg.

[]: Re: Help Purchasing Neon Genesis             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Wed Feb 18, 2015 6:39 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Wishful thinking.

[]:             Quote Post by Bonifatus » Wed Feb 18, 2015 7:10 pm Well waiting for funimation to do anything certainly seems live a very long term option... What would be the differences with say this version over the platinum edition? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Feb 18, 2015 7:13 pm ^ This one ist most probably not legit.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Feb 18, 2015 7:26 pm “Vol. 1” has 26 episodes and all 5 movies (Death, EoE, and NTE 1-3)? Will Vol. 2 have Eva Shin and all of the Eva@School episodes? Or will it just be that infamous Eva Hentai animation? (I say this knowing it’s a bootleg.)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 18, 2015 8:03 pm Bonifatus wrote: What would be the differences with say this version over the platinum edition? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Cirk » Wed Feb 18, 2015 8:30 pm You could always check your local secondhand media stores, too. (Half Price Books?) That's how I got my copy of the Platinum Thinpak last summer for $60.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Feb 18, 2015 8:51 pm Every so often I find a legit thin-pack at a pawn shop. It can’t hurt to look.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Feb 19, 2015 1:45 am ^I've tried used media stores, thrift shops...Keep finding the Perfect Collection DVDs. Funny, last weekend I saw the multipack Bonifatus pointed out at Japan Video in Japantown. It's got Malay and Thai subs IIRC. Later when I returned to the store someone was in the process of purchasing said multipack.

[]:             Quote Post by Bonifatus » Wed Feb 25, 2015 11:04 pm Definitely looking for a region one release. On a related note is it safe to assume that the copies of End of Evangelion/Death and Rebirth that have both on one disk and are prolific on eBay are bootlegs like this one: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Feb 26, 2015 1:32 am Other signs that you're looking at a bootleg: -Region free/0 -Subs in multiple Asian languages -Packaging you've never seen before on other releases for the same content/modified packaging

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Feb 26, 2015 2:25 am - Series and Films in same package.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Feb 26, 2015 12:54 pm Bonifatus wrote: is it safe to assume that the copies of End of Evangelion/Death and Rebirth that have both on one disk and are prolific on eBay are bootlegs like this one:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 26, 2015 4:06 pm If you're just looking for R1 DVDs, here's a real easy way to avoid bootlegs: Don't buy it unless the package clearly says "ADV Films" or "Manga Entertainment" on it. Reputable sellers are aware of bootlegs and will provide pictures clear enough to let you know you're getting the real thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Thu Feb 26, 2015 4:12 pm AR-99 wrote: Other signs that you're looking at a bootleg: -Region free/0 -Subs in multiple Asian languages -Packaging you've never seen before on other releases for the same content/modified packaging

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Feb 27, 2015 4:20 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Or will it just be that infamous Eva Hentai animation? (I say this knowing it’s a bootleg.)

[]: Help Purchasing Neon Genesis Evangelion             Quote Post by Bonifatus » Wed Feb 18, 2015 1:31 pm I'm a fairly new fan to the series and was looking to purchase Neon Genesis on DVD. Due to price constraints I'm leaning towards the Platinum Holiday Edition. I was wondering what I should look for to ensure I'm not buying a Bootleg.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks
